items.text: Convert scattered founder notes into explicit context markers.
Keep v0.1 focused on deterministic Markdown extraction.
Add snapshot-quality exporter tests.
Implement the Markdown scanner and parser.
Keep plugin work limited to roadmap documentation for now.
Revisit old weekly-review actions before publishing v0.1.
Write the first README quickstart.
Document future memory diff and source provenance scoring.
Early users are likely to be Obsidian power users and technical builders.
A CLI-first foundation is easier to test and publish as open-source infrastructure.
Human-readable Markdown is the right first export format.
Build the CLI before the Obsidian plugin.
Treat Obsidian as the first adapter, not the core product.
GotSaeng OS should be framework-first and plugin-second.
The compiler belongs in packages/core so future adapters do not own the product.
GotSaeng OS means both the Korean internet phrase "God Life" and the reinterpretation "Got 生."
GotSaeng OS is a human-in-the-loop context operating system.
GotSaeng OS is a local-first context compiler.
GotSaeng OS is not an AI note app.
The Obsidian plugin is an adapter for v0.2+, not the v0.1 core.
The first compiler should produce PROJECT_CONTEXT.md, MEMORY_SNAPSHOT.md, and decision/action/risk/question outputs.
Model-ready context packs should include source paths so humans can audit claims.
A raw chat becomes useful when the compiler extracts durable memory instead of preserving every message.
ADHD-aware workflows can help anyone managing fragmented attention and long-running goals.
The real product is portable context infrastructure, not a note app.
A boring compiler is a stronger base than a flashy integration.
Stale context is not failure; it is a recovery surface.
Context over automation is the strongest design constraint.
LLMs are powerful, but long-running work loses continuity when source notes, chat logs,
decisions, risks, and execution traces are scattered.
Which parts of a chat export should become durable project memory?
What is the smallest context pack that feels useful in a real handoff?
Which stale items are still relevant after the compiler lands?
Should future context packs include confidence metadata?
A generic productivity dashboard could dilute the compiler-first identity.
Building the plugin first could trap the architecture inside Obsidian assumptions.
Old open actions can stay invisible unless stale context is surfaced.
Semantic contradiction detection is out of scope for v0.1.
